Package org.rodinp.core.basis

Class RodinElement

java.lang.Object
org.eclipse.core.runtime.PlatformObject
org.rodinp.core.basis.RodinElement
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.eclipse.core.runtime.IAdaptable, IRodinElement
Direct Known Subclasses:
InternalElement, Openable
public abstract class RodinElement extends org.eclipse.core.runtime.PlatformObject implements IRodinElement
Root of Rodin element handle hierarchy.
Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • IRodinElement

  • Method Details

    • createProblemMarker

      public void createProblemMarker(IRodinProblem problem, Object... args) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Creates a new Rodin Problem marker for this element.

      The new marker is attached to the underlying resource of this element. Its marker type is RodinMarkerUtil.RODIN_PROBLEM_MARKER.

      Specified by:
      createProblemMarker in interface IRodinElement
      Parameters:
      problem - problem to attach to the new marker
      args - arguments to the problem
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this method fails. Reasons include:
      • This element does not exist.
      See Also:
      • RodinMarkerUtil

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Returns true if this handle represents the same Rodin element as the given handle. By default, two handles represent the same element if they are identical or if they represent the same type of element, have equal names and parents.

      If a subclass has other requirements for equality, this method must be overridden.

      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      See Also:
      • Object.equals(java.lang.Object)

    • exists

      public abstract boolean exists()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns whether this Rodin element exists in the database.

      Rodin elements are handle objects that may or may not be backed by an actual element. Rodin elements that are backed by an actual element are said to "exist", and this method returns true. For Rodin elements, it is always the case that if the element exists, then its parent also exists (provided it has one). Moreover, for any element which is not a stable file snapshot, the parent includes the element as one of its children. It is therefore possible to navigate to any existing Rodin element (except stable file snapshots) from the root of the Rodin database along a chain of existing Rodin elements. On the other hand, a stable snapshot file never shows up among the children of its parent element.

      The fact that an openable element exists gives no guarantee upon whether it can be opened. For instance, a malformed Rodin file may exist, while it can never be opened by the database (because it contains invalid XML).

      A call to this method might open the ancestors of this element, but will never open this element itself.

      Specified by:
      exists in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      true if this element exists in the Rodin database, and false if this element does not exist

    • getAncestor

      public <T extends IRodinElement> T getAncestor(IElementType<T> ancestorType)
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the first ancestor of this Rodin element that has the given type. Returns null if no such an ancestor can be found.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getAncestor in interface IRodinElement
      Parameters:
      ancestorType - the type of the ancestor to look up
      Returns:
      the first ancestor of this Rodin element that has the given type, or null if no such an ancestor can be found
      See Also:
      • IRodinElement

    • getChildren

      public RodinElement[] getChildren() throws RodinDBException
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      See Also:
      • IParent

    • getChildrenOfType

      public <T extends IRodinElement> T[] getChildrenOfType(IElementType<T> type) throws RodinDBException
      Throws:
      RodinDBException
      See Also:
      • IParent

    • getElementInfo

      public RodinElementInfo getElementInfo() throws RodinDBException
      Returns the info for this handle. If this element is not already open, it and all of its parents are opened. Does not return null.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if the element is not present or not accessible

    • getElementInfo

      public abstract RodinElementInfo getElementInfo(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Returns the info for this handle. If this element is not already open, it and all of its parents are opened. Does not return null.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if the element is not present or not accessible

      public abstract String getElementName()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the name of this element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getElementName in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the element name
      See Also:

    • getElementType

      public abstract IElementType<? extends IRodinElement> getElementType()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getElementType in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:

    • getHandleFromMemento

      public IRodinElement getHandleFromMemento(MementoTokenizer memento)

    • getHandleIdentifier

      public String getHandleIdentifier()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns a string representation of this element handle. The format of the string is not specified; however, the identifier is stable across workspace sessions, and can be used to recreate this handle via the RodinCore.valueOf(String) method.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getHandleIdentifier in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the string handle identifier
      See Also:
      • RodinCore.valueOf(String)

    • getRodinDB

      public IRodinDB getRodinDB()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the Rodin database.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getRodinDB in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the Rodin database
      See Also:
      • IRodinElement

    • getRodinProject

      public IRodinProject getRodinProject()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the Rodin project this element is contained in, or null if this element is not contained in any Rodin project (for instance, the IRodinDB is not contained in any Rodin project).

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getRodinProject in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the containing Rodin project, or null if this element is not contained in a Rodin project
      See Also:
      • IRodinElement

    • getOpenable

      public abstract Openable getOpenable()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the first openable parent. If this element is openable, the element itself is returned. Returns null if this element doesn't have an openable parent.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getOpenable in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the first openable parent or null if this element doesn't have an openable parent.

    • getParent

      public RodinElement getParent()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the element directly containing this element, or null if this element has no parent.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getParent in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the parent element, or null if this element has no parent
      See Also:
      • IRodinElement

    • getPrimaryElement

      public IRodinElement getPrimaryElement()

      public IRodinElement getPrimaryElement(boolean checkOwner)

      public org.eclipse.core.runtime.jobs.ISchedulingRule getSchedulingRule()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the scheduling rule associated with this Rodin element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getSchedulingRule in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the scheduling rule associated with this Rodin element

    • hasChildren

      public abstract boolean hasChildren() throws RodinDBException
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Returns the hash code for this Rodin element. By default, the hash code for an element is a combination of its name and parent's hash code. Elements with other requirements must override this method.
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object

    • isAncestorOf

      public boolean isAncestorOf(IRodinElement e)
      Returns true if this element is an ancestor of the given element, otherwise false.
      Specified by:
      isAncestorOf in interface IRodinElement
      Parameters:
      e - the element to test as a descendent
      Returns:
      true iff this element is an ancestor of the given element

    • isReadOnly

      public boolean isReadOnly()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns whether this Rodin element is read-only. An element can be read-only for two reasons: either it corresponds to a resource which is read-only, or it is an internal element that belongs to a stable snapshot of a Rodin file, or the stable snapshot itself.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      isReadOnly in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      true iff this element is read-only

    • isRoot

      public final boolean isRoot()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns whether this Rodin element is a root element. A root element is an internal element which is the child of a non-internal element (for instance, the top-level element of a Rodin file).

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      isRoot in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      true iff this element is a root element

    • newNotPresentException

      public RodinDBException newNotPresentException()
      Creates and returns a new not present exception for this element.

      public RodinDBException newRodinDBException(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IStatus status)
      Creates and returns a new Rodin database exception for this element with the given status.
